headline: ADP Report On Job Anxiety May Support Demand For HR Data Tools
summary: ADP Research released its "Today at Work 2026" report, highlighting that only 22% of global workers feel secure in their jobs. The findings point to widespread job security anxiety despite low unemployment rates, with particular concern among lower-paid and frontline workers. The report emphasizes how artificial intelligence and demographic shifts are reshaping worker sentiment and expectations. For investors following Automatic Data Processing (NasdaqGS:ADP), this report goes straight to...

headline: The AI Paradox: Heavy AI Usage Makes Workers Feel Less Productive
summary: The ADP survey supports other data that show generative AI spend is rising, but direct productivity gains from the technology aren’t materializing yet.

raw.headline: The AI Paradox: Heavy AI Usage Makes Workers Feel Less Productive
raw.summary: The ADP survey supports other data that show generative AI spend is rising, but direct productivity gains from the technology aren’t materializing yet.

headline: Only 22% of global workers believe their jobs are safe, ADP finds
summary: Investing.com -- Only 22% of global workers strongly believe their jobs are safe from elimination, according to a new report from ADP Research released Wednesday, revealing widespread anxiety despite historically low unemployment rates worldwide.

raw.headline: Only 22% of global workers believe their jobs are safe, ADP finds
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Only 22% of global workers strongly believe their jobs are safe from elimination, according to a new report from ADP Research released Wednesday, revealing widespread anxiety despite historically low unemployment rates worldwide.

headline: ADP Research: Only 22% of Workers Confident Their Job is Safe from Elimination, Underscoring the Importance of Talent Strategies that Prepare Employees for the Future
summary: Only 22% of global workers strongly agreed their job was safe from elimination according to Today at Work 2026, Issue 1 from ADP Research. Anxiety over job security was particularly acute among lower-paid, repetitive task workers and people at the bottom of the management hierarchy. These findings, among others, emphasize the opportunity for employers to reimagine their talent strategies and provide clarity to their employees as advances in artificial intelligence and changing demographics bring

headline: ADP to Announce Third Quarter Fiscal 2026 Financial Results on April 29, 2026
summary: ADP (Nasdaq: ADP), a global leader in HR and payroll solutions, is scheduled to release its financial results for the third fiscal quarter ending March 31, 2026 before the opening of the Nasdaq on April 29, 2026.
